repro.description: KnexScheduleRepository.writeChanged batched-CASE path has CASE branches for scheduled_at, dur, date, day, time, but NO branch for split_ordinal/split_total. When a drift-fix delta (runSchedule.js:1295-1303) carrying dur + split_ordinal + split_total is routed through writeChanged via the dur trigger, dur is persisted but split_ordinal and split_total are silently dropped — desyncing split-chunk metadata for every user.
steps: Seed a task_instance with split_ordinal=1, split_total=2, dur=30
Create a delta with dbUpdate={dur:45, split_ordinal:2, split_total:3, updated_at}
Read back the instance — split_ordinal and split_total will still be 1 and 2 (dropped), only dur updated to 45
repro.evidence: runSchedule.js:1298 drift-fix CASE update writes split_ordinal/split_total/dur alongside flag fields. KnexScheduleRepository.writeChanged batched path (line ~112-170) handles dur CASE but has no split_ordinal/split_total CASE.

root_cause.hypothesis: The batched-CASE path in writeChanged was implemented with CASE branches for scheduled_at, dur, date, day, time but missed split_ordinal and split_total. When a drift-fix delta includes these fields alongside dur, the batched path writes dur via CASE but silently drops split_ordinal/split_total because they have no CASE branch and are not in the generic otherUpdates path (they're in INSTANCE_UPDATE_FIELDS but the batched path only processes known CASE columns).
root_cause.fix: Add a forEach loop for ['split_ordinal', 'split_total'] mirroring the dur CASE pattern, plus add both fields to INSTANCE_UPDATE_FIELDS in tasks-write.js.
